user_prompt: read `lib\WifiManager\CloudUpload.h` and do what's in the comment
agent_reply: - Starts a FreeRTOS task pinned to the current core.
- Scans the microSD recursively for pending `*.rec` files.
- Checks `/cloud_history.txt` by streaming it each time, not loading it into RAM.
- Supports HTTP and HTTPS. HTTPS currently uses `WiFiClientSecure::setInsecure()` so it works without certificate setup.
- Retries each file up to 3 times.
- Appends errors as non-valid history lines starting with timestamp.
- Provides thread-safe status with file and byte progress.

user_prompt: cleanly remove the requirement of password for cloud uploads, the security design has changed
agent_reply: Implemented. Cloud upload destinations no longer require or store a password/salt.
- Legacy non-secure upload hash is now filename + timestamp only.
- Secure upload path still uses `Aegis::hmacSha256` with the master key.
- Added secure NVS migration for old network config blobs that still had the cloud password field, dropping that field and saving the new layout.
Build passed. I restored the generated `src/version.c` churn afterward.

user_prompt: the document `lib\Security\security-levels.md` is up to date and reflects what I want. `lib\Security\security-policies-specifications.md` reflects the maximum security practices but `security-levels.md` has priority.
do a clean up of mistakes when I did the switching of the `#define`
also, get rid of any code that deals with `legacy` nvs storage, we do not need to care about that until development has reached some form of release
agent_reply: Cleaned up the `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY_LEVEL` switch fallout and removed legacy NVS migration paths.
- Removed old `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY` branches and old linked-list compatibility paths from Wi-Fi/Bluetooth config code.
- Removed Wi-Fi and Bluetooth legacy NVS migration handling.
- Fixed fixed-entry structs fallout: removed `next_node` usage and cloud `name` usage.
- Level 0: `/get_cfg` and `/set_cfg` use plain JSON, cloud upload auth uses per-cloud password.
- Level 1+: config transport stays session encrypted, cloud upload auth uses `network-key`.
- FTP only starts when `BUILD_FTP_SERVER` is set and `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY_LEVEL <= 0`.
- Boot now loads Wi-Fi/Bluetooth JSON only for level 0; higher levels load NVS directly.
- Cloud list labels now use URL instead of removed cloud name.
- `git diff --check` on the files I edited passed.
- All four got through compilation and failed only at the known link-stage IRAM0 issue: `.iram0.text` overflowed by `2104 bytes`.

Global `git diff --check` still reports trailing whitespace in `.vscode/extensions.json`, `inc/conf.h`, and `lib/Security/security-levels.md`; I left those alone since they look like your existing macro/doc edit churn.

- Removed old `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY` branches and old linked-list compatibility paths from Wi-Fi/Bluetooth config code.
- Removed Wi-Fi and Bluetooth legacy NVS migration handling.
- Fixed fixed-entry structs fallout: removed `next_node` usage and cloud `name` usage.
- Level 0: `/get_cfg` and `/set_cfg` use plain JSON, cloud upload auth uses per-cloud password.
- Level 1+: config transport stays session encrypted, cloud upload auth uses `network-key`.
- FTP only starts when `BUILD_FTP_SERVER` is set and `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY_LEVEL <= 0`.
- Boot now loads Wi-Fi/Bluetooth JSON only for level 0; higher levels load NVS directly.
- Cloud list labels now use URL instead of removed cloud name.
- `git diff --check` on the files I edited passed.

user_prompt: `void CloudUpload::taskMain()` needs to be have the internals of the `for (PendingFile* item = head; item; item = item->next)` loop be broken out in a way that allows for easy calling/testing for just one file
so whatever function you've broken that out into needs another layer of caller that specifies a file, builds a PendingFile structure, and frees the structure when done
also `WiFiClientSecure` needs to be actually secure as much as possible, the destination needs to be authentic
`ClockAgent` must make sure `WiFiClientSecure` works as intended
agent_reply: - Added `CloudUpload::uploadSingleFile(destination, path, timeout_ms)` for easy one-file testing.
- Broke the per-file upload work out into `uploadPendingFile(...)`, shared by both the normal scan loop and the single-file wrapper.
- The single-file path builds a `PendingFile`, calls the shared uploader, then frees it.
- HTTPS is now required for cloud uploads; `http://` is rejected.
- `WiFiClientSecure` now uses the ESP x509 cert bundle via `setCACertBundle(...)`, with hostname verification handled by the secure client connect path.
- `ClockAgent` now pushes its RTC/managed time into system time and exposes `ensureSystemTimeForTls()`, which CloudUpload calls before TLS handshakes.
I did not run PlatformIO, since you said it was busy. Static checks only.
